name: Porter Cloud Application Deployment Platform API
what_it_does: Cloud application deployment platform with REST API — enabling development teams to deploy applications to their own AWS, GCP, or Azure accounts using Kubernetes without managing Kubernetes directly, with Heroku-like simplicity (git push deploys, add-ons, environment variables), GitHub Actions integration, preview environments, and infrastructure-as-code via Porter's configuration model.
use_cases: Deploying applications to AWS/GCP/Azure Kubernetes via Porter API for deployment automation
Managing Porter applications, environments, and add-ons via API for GitOps integration
Creating preview environments for pull requests via Porter API for CI/CD workflow
Querying deployment status and application health via Porter API for deployment monitoring
Managing Porter clusters and resources via API for infrastructure automation
not_for: Teams that want fully managed Kubernetes (Porter deploys to your own cloud — you pay cloud bills)
Simple static hosting (use Netlify or Vercel for static sites)
Fully serverless applications (use AWS Lambda or Vercel for serverless workloads)
best_when: A startup or growing team outgrowing Heroku wants Kubernetes-powered deployment on their own cloud accounts — with Heroku-like simplicity but without Heroku's pricing and vendor lock-in.
avoid_when: You need fully managed infrastructure (cloud bills go to you), prefer serverless, have a small team where Heroku's simplicity is sufficient, or need advanced Kubernetes features beyond what Porter exposes.

auth.notes: API token from Porter account. Bearer token auth. Project-scoped access. Webhook for deployment events. REST API for application and cluster management. GitHub Actions integration for CI/CD. CLI (porter-dev/porter CLI) for local deployment. Infrastructure provisioned on user's AWS/GCP/Azure account. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; all plans include infrastructure cost on user's cloud
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter $19/month + infrastructure costs; Growth $49/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: San Francisco company. Founded 2020 by Justin Gage and Shikhar Bahl. Raised $5M seed (2021). YCombinator S20. Heroku-killer positioning. Open source CLI (GitHub: porter-dev/porter). Kubernetes-on-your-cloud model — all infrastructure runs in user's AWS/GCP/Azure. Preview environments (per-PR deploys) as key developer experience feature. GitHub Actions native integration. Porter charges for the platform; cloud bills are separate. Growing engineering community. Competition from Railway, Render, and Northflank.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for application and cluster management. API token auth. Good developer documentation. Webhook for deployment events. GitHub Actions integration documented. Infrastructure in user's cloud — data residency controlled by user. Rate limits not documented. YCombinator-backed startup with strong developer experience focus.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Application reads fully idempotent. Deployment reads idempotent by deployment ID. Cluster reads idempotent. Application deployment creation requires idempotency at CI layer.
known_agent_gotchas: Infrastructure runs in user's cloud account — AWS/GCP/Azure billing is separate from Porter fees
Kubernetes management abstracted — direct kubectl access possible but Porter manages desired state
No free tier — must pay from day one
Rate limits not documented — test before high-frequency deployment API calls
Preview environments require GitHub integration — separate from Porter API

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 compliant. User-controlled data residency (infrastructure in own cloud). API token auth. YCombinator startup with security focus. Porter has IAM access to user's cloud account — review minimal permissions. Application secrets managed in Porter — encrypted at rest. Kubernetes RBAC for cluster access control. Porter's cloud access should be scoped to minimum necessary IAM permissions. Network policies via Kubernetes NetworkPolicy.
